subject: Re: repeat massages
body:
I thinkwe should also recite the history of the Mann act trafficking, and the history of these statutes.. , since they have asked about gifts,, they could use that fact as part of the so called inducement chain.. re 2423, the case law specifically says that the sex should not be merely incidental to the trip.. they would argue that I saw the same group of girls, often, therefore,  at least more tha an incidental reason.. THANKS

----- Original Message ----
From: Mitch Webber <mwebber@law.harvard.edu>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Cc: dersh@law.harvard.edu
Sent: Thursday, November 9, 2006 10:24:50 PM
Subject: Re: repeat massages


Dear Mr. Epstein,

    I missed something.  Alan also wanted me to send you the two paragraphs 
below, which are the beginning of an argument we have to make coving the 
masseuses who returned to you home on several occasions.

Sincerely,
Mitch

--------

    The "persuasion, inducement, enticement or coercion" element against 
Epstein is actually weakened by the fact that the masseuses regularly 
returned to Epstein's home for more massages.  There are no allegations 
whatsoever that Epstein pressured or threatened anyone to do anything she 
didn't want to do.  They wanted to come back.   If Epstein did not 
persuade, induce, entice or coerce them the first time, it is nonsensical 
to believe that he did in subsequent interactions, when the girls returned 
knowing precisely what how everyone behaved in Epstein's massage room.  As 
noted earlier, "[t]he underlying criminal conduct ... is the persuasion, 
inducement, enticement or coercion of the minor rather than the sex act 
itself."  No matter what happened between Epstein and his masseuses, the 
fact that they kept coming back militates powerfully against any sort of 
pressure on Epstein's part.
    
    Even if there was an original "persuasion," it took place entirely within 
Florida -- indeed, within JE's own home, and not across state lines.  That 
is not a federal crime.  Such initial, intrastate "persuasion" does not 
metastasize into an interstate federal crime simply because JE's agent 
schedule repeat sessions, since such scheduling does not constitute 
inducement by use of the modalities of interstate commerce.
